---
title: "Guillermo Fandos"
subtitle: "Spatial Ecologist & Conservation Scientist"
description: "Guillermo Fandos - spatial ecologist and Assistant Professor at Universidad Complutense de Madrid. Research on animal dispersal, species distribution modelling, and biodiversity forecasting."
image: images/avatar.jpg
image-alt: "Guillermo Fandos, spatial ecologist"
toc: false
about:
  id: hero
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:gfandos@ucm.es
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.es/citations?user=8zRbuNgAAAAJ
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/guifandos
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1579-9444
page-layout: full
---

:::{#hero}

I am a spatial ecologist interested in why animals move, how we can monitor biodiversity, and how species respond to global change. My work sits at the crossroads of fieldwork, large-scale data synthesis, and quantitative modelling --- from ringing birds across Europe to building predictive tools for threatened species.

I work as **Assistant Professor** at [Universidad Complutense de Madrid](https://www.ucm.es/bee/directorio/?id=29786), where I combine research with teaching. Always happy to hear from potential collaborators and students.

:::

---

## Research Questions

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .research-card}
### Where do animals go --- and why?
We study how individuals move across landscapes, from daily foraging to lifetime dispersal. Why do two birds of the same species behave so differently? Understanding this helps predict range shifts under global change.

[Movement & Dispersal](research/dispersal.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .research-card}
### Can we forecast biodiversity?
Combining citizen science, standardised surveys, and hierarchical models to monitor and model species distributions at large scales --- and anticipate what comes next.

[Monitoring & Modelling](research/forecasting.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .research-card}
### How do we translate science into action?
From large carnivore management to habitat connectivity, we work to make ecological models useful for real conservation decisions.

[Conservation & Global Change](research/conservation.qmd){.card-link}
:::

:::

---

## Projects {#projects}

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .project-card}
::: {.project-badge}
PI · 2024--2026
:::
### [INTRADISP](projects/intradisp.qmd)
Why do individuals of the same species disperse so differently? Synthesising European bird ringing data to find out.

::: {.project-funder}
UCM
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .project-card}
::: {.project-badge}
Team member · 2024--2027
:::
### [RIMed-Fauna](projects/remined.qmd)
How does wildlife cope with the extreme seasonality of Mediterranean rivers? Camera trapping and SDMs in National Parks.

::: {.project-funder}
Red de Parques Nacionales
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .project-card}
::: {.project-badge}
Team member · 2025--2027
:::
### SHAREPOINT
Social organisation and parasite sharing among rainforest understory birds in French Guiana.

::: {.project-funder}
CEBA
:::
:::

:::

[Past projects & applied contracts](projects/index.qmd){.see-all}

---

## News

- **Feb 2026** --- New paper in *Communications Biology*: [Dispersal syndromes across the tree of life](https://www.nature.com/articles/s42003-026-09676-x) (Fandos et al. 2026)
- **Feb 2026** --- In Vienna for the [EUFLYNET](https://www.cost.eu/actions/CA22117/) COST Action meeting on bird migration research
- **2025** --- Welcome to **David Notario** and **Claudia Mediavilla** joining as researchers

---

::: {.grid .contact-block}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Get in touch

I'm always open to new collaborations, especially if you're interested in dispersal ecology, species distribution modelling, or conservation applications. If you're a student looking for a thesis project, don't hesitate to write.

[gfandos@ucm.es](mailto:gfandos@ucm.es)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
**Guillermo Fandos**
Dept. Biodiversidad, Ecologia y Evolucion
Universidad Complutense de Madrid
C/ Jose Antonio Novais 12, 28040 Madrid

![](images/logos/ucm.svg){width=100 fig-alt="UCM logo"}
:::

:::
